kaos_elements.name: Safety Goal
kaos_elements.description: Safety objective for AD: The system must minimize "Jerk" (rate of change of acceleration) to ensure passenger comfort
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): The system must minimize "Jerk" (rate of change of acceleration) to ensure passenger comfort
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): - Curvature constraints ($kappa$) must be satisfied to prevent rollover or tire slip
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): - Non-convex obstacle constraints must be handled using iterative quadratic programming (QP)
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): [Conflict Scenario for Negotiation]
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): - The SafetyAgent prioritizes "Passive Safety" and strictly enforcing safe distances and MRM execution
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): - The EfficiencyAgent prioritizes "Smoothness" and "Speed" using the QP function to minimize Jerk
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

steps.analysis_text: Round 1: SafetyAgent submits model for dialectic review by EfficiencyAgent.
steps.analysis: Round 1: SafetyAgent submits model for dialectic review by EfficiencyAgent.

steps.analysis_text: The current safety and efficiency requirements for the Baidu Apollo Autonomous Driving System reflect a potential conflict between the SafetyAgent and EfficiencyAgent. While the SafetyAgent prioritizes minimizing Jerk for passenger comfort, the EfficiencyAgent's requirements suggest that emergency maneuvers may necessitate relaxing these smoothness constraints. This dichotomy highlights the need for architectural negotiation to effectively balance safety and efficiency in real-time scenarios.
steps.analysis: The current safety and efficiency requirements for the Baidu Apollo Autonomous Driving System reflect a potential conflict between the SafetyAgent and EfficiencyAgent. While the SafetyAgent prioritizes minimizing Jerk for passenger comfort, the EfficiencyAgent's requirements suggest that emergency maneuvers may necessitate relaxing these smoothness constraints. This dichotomy highlights the need for architectural negotiation to effectively balance safety and efficiency in real-time scenarios.
steps.feedback: The negotiation should focus on aligning the safety goals with efficiency metrics, especially in emergency situations where Jerk constraints may be compromised. It is essential to define clear protocols for how the system should respond to emergencies without undermining safety standards.

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Goal
kaos_elements.description: Trustworthiness objective for AD: ODD (Operational Design Domain):
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): ODD (Operational Design Domain):
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (auditability): - The system operates in specific urban and highway scenarios
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for auditability

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (integrity guarantees): It must continuously monitor ODD compliance and request human takeover (HMI warning) if boundaries are reached
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for integrity guarantees

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): [Efficiency & Planning Requirements - Based on EM Motion Planner]
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (auditability): Multiobjective Optimization:
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for auditability

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (integrity guarantees): - The planner must simultaneously optimize for three conflicting objectives: Safety (collision avoidance), Smoothness (passenger comfort), and Speed (reaching destination)
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for integrity guarantees

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 7
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): Path-Speed Decoupling (EM Framework):
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

steps.analysis_text: The negotiation between TrustworthinessAgent and ResponsibilityAgent has identified several areas of potential conflict regarding the safety and responsibility requirements for the Baidu Apollo Autonomous Driving System. The Trustworthiness Requirements emphasize the necessity of ensuring security assurance, auditability, and integrity guarantees across the system's operations, particularly in the context of the Operational Design Domain (ODD). Meanwhile, the Responsibility Requirements focus on regulatory accountability, stakeholder transparency, and ethical compliance, particularly concerning the path-speed decoupling and trajectory optimization processes.
steps.analysis: The negotiation between TrustworthinessAgent and ResponsibilityAgent has identified several areas of potential conflict regarding the safety and responsibility requirements for the Baidu Apollo Autonomous Driving System. The Trustworthiness Requirements emphasize the necessity of ensuring security assurance, auditability, and integrity guarantees across the system's operations, particularly in the context of the Operational Design Domain (ODD). Meanwhile, the Responsibility Requirements focus on regulatory accountability, stakeholder transparency, and ethical compliance, particularly concerning the path-speed decoupling and trajectory optimization processes.
steps.feedback: Both agents need to collaborate to ensure that the trustworthiness objectives do not compromise the responsibility goals. For instance, the focus on optimizing for safety, smoothness, and speed must align with the regulatory and ethical standards outlined by the ResponsibilityAgent. It is essential to find common ground where safety measures do not impede the system's accountability and transparency.

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Goal
kaos_elements.description: Responsibility objective for AD: Path-Speed Decoupling (EM Framework):
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (regulatory accountability): Path-Speed Decoupling (EM Framework):
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for regulatory accountability

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (stakeholder transparency): - The system must use the Expectation-Maximization (EM) framework
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for stakeholder transparency

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (ethical compliance): - Step 1 (E-step): Generate an optimal path in the Frenet Frame (SL-Graph)
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for ethical compliance

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (regulatory accountability): - Step 2 (M-step): Generate an optimal speed profile in the Station-Time Frame (ST-Graph)
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for regulatory accountability

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (stakeholder transparency): Hard Constraints (Mathematical):
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for stakeholder transparency

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (ethical compliance): - Trajectory smoothness is critical
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for ethical compliance

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 7
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (regulatory accountability): The system must minimize "Jerk" (rate of change of acceleration) to ensure passenger comfort
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for regulatory accountability
